title: Cisco HSRP First-Hop Redundancy
description: Configure Hot Standby Router Protocol (HSRP) on multilayer switch SVIs with virtual IP, priority, preemption, interface tracking, and verification.

tasks.prompt: Enter Global Configuration mode on distribution switch DSW1 to configure HSRP.
tasks.explanation: HSRP is configured on Layer 3 interfaces—typically SVIs on multilayer switches acting as default gateways.

tasks.prompt: Enter Interface Configuration mode for the VLAN 10 SVI.
tasks.explanation: HSRP provides a virtual default gateway IP shared between two physical routers or switches serving the same VLAN.

tasks.prompt: Assign physical IP address 192.168.10.2/24 to the VLAN 10 SVI on DSW1.
tasks.explanation: Each HSRP router needs a unique physical IP on the interface. Clients use the separate virtual IP as their default gateway.

tasks.prompt: Configure HSRP group 10 with virtual IP address 192.168.10.1 on VLAN 10.
tasks.explanation: The standby IP is the virtual gateway address shared by all routers in HSRP group 10. Only the active router responds to ARP for this IP.

tasks.prompt: Set HSRP group 10 priority to 110 on DSW1 to make it the preferred active router.
tasks.explanation: The router with the highest priority becomes active. Default priority is 100; raising it above the peer ensures DSW1 is preferred.

tasks.prompt: Enable preemption on HSRP group 10 so DSW1 reclaims active status when it recovers.
tasks.explanation: Without preempt, a recovered higher-priority router stays in standby. Preempt allows it to take over the active role automatically.

tasks.prompt: Track interface GigabitEthernet0/1 in HSRP group 10 and decrease priority by 25 if the link goes down.
tasks.explanation: Interface tracking lowers HSRP priority when an uplink fails, triggering failover to the standby router before traffic is black-holed.

tasks.prompt: Display HSRP group status for VLAN 10 including active/standby roles and timers.
tasks.explanation: Show standby confirms which router is active, the virtual IP, priority, preempt state, and tracked interface status.
